options.name: progression_balancing
options.description:
A system that can move progression earlier, to try and prevent the player from getting stuck and bored early.

A lower setting means more getting stuck. A higher setting means less getting stuck.

options.name: accessibility
options.description:
Set rules for reachability of your items/locations.

**Full:** ensure everything can be reached and acquired.

**Minimal:** ensure what is needed to reach your goal can be acquired.

options.name: final_rest_door_key
options.description:
Determines what grants the player the Final Rest Door Key.

Sephiroth: Defeat Sephiroth

Unknown: Defeat Unknown

Postcards: Turn in an amount of postcards in Traverse Town

Final Ansem: Enter End of the World and defeat Ansem as normal

Puppies: Rescue and return an amount of puppies in Traverse Town

Final Rest: Open the chest in End of the World Final Rest

options.name: end_of_the_world_unlock
options.description:
Determines how End of the World is unlocked.

Item: You can receive an item called "End of the World" which unlocks the world

Lucky Emblems: A certain amount of lucky emblems are required to unlock End of the World, which is defined in your options

options.name: required_lucky_emblems_eotw
options.display_name: Lucky Emblems to Open End of the World
options.description: If End of the World Unlock is set to "Lucky Emblems", determines the number of Lucky Emblems required.

options.name: required_lucky_emblems_door
options.display_name: Lucky Emblems to Open Final Rest Door
options.description: If Final Rest Door Key is set to "Lucky Emblems", determines the number of Lucky Emblems required.

options.name: lucky_emblems_in_pool
options.description: Determines the number of Lucky Emblems in the item pool.

options.name: required_postcards
options.description: If "Final Rest Door Key" is set to "Postcards", defines how many postcards are required.

options.name: required_puppies
options.description: If "Final Rest Door Key" is set to "Puppies", defines how many puppies are required.

options.name: destiny_islands
options.description:
If on, Adds a Destiny Islands item and a number of Raft Materials items to the pool.

When "Destiny Islands" is found, Traverse Town will have an additional place to land - Seashore.

"Raft Materials" allow progress into Day 2 and to Homecoming (Final Bosses).  The amount is defined in Day 2 Materials and Homecoming Materials.

options.name: day_2_materials
options.description: The amount of Raft Materials required to access Day 2.

options.name: homecoming_materials
options.description: The amount of Raft Materials required to access Homecoming (Final Bosses).

options.name: materials_in_pool
options.description: The amount of Raft Materials required to access Homecoming (Final Bosses).

options.name: super_bosses
options.description: Toggle whether to include checks behind Super Bosses.

options.name: atlantica
options.description: Toggle whether to include checks in Atlantica.

options.name: hundred_acre_wood
options.description: Toggle whether to include checks in the 100 Acre Wood.

options.name: cups
options.description:
Determines which cups have their locations added to the multiworld.

Please note that the cup items will still appear in the multiworld even if set to off, as they are required to challenge Sephiroth.

Off: All cup locations are removed

Cups: Phil, Pegasus, and Hercules cups are included

Hades Cup: Hades Cup is included in addition to Phil, Pegasus, and Hercules cups. If Super Bosses are enabled, then Ice Titan is included

options.name: randomize_emblem_pieces
options.description: If off, the Hollow Bastion emblem pieces are in their vanilla locations.

options.name: randomize_postcards
options.description:
Determines how Postcards are randomized

All: All Postcards are randomized

Chests: Only the 3 Postcards in chests are randomized

Vanilla: Postcards are in their original location

options.name: jungle_slider
options.description: Determines whether checks are behind the Jungle Slider minigame.

options.name: exp_multiplier
options.description: Determines the multiplier to apply to EXP gained.

options.name: level_checks
options.description: Determines the maximum level for which checks can be obtained.

options.name: slot_2_level_checks
options.description:
Determines how many levels have an additional item.

If Remote Items is OFF, these checks will only contain abilities or items for other players.

options.name: force_stats_on_levels
options.display_name: Force Stats on Level Starting From
options.description:
If this value is less than the value for Level Checks, this determines the minimum level from which only stat ups are obtained at level up locations.

For example, if you want to be able to find any multiworld item from levels 2-50, then just stat ups for levels 51-100, set this value to 51.

options.name: strength_increase
options.description:
Determines the number of Strength Increases to add to the multiworld.

The randomizer will add all stat ups defined here into a pool and choose up to 99 to add to the multiworld.

Accessory Slot Increases and Item Slot Increases are prioritized first, then the remaining items (up to 99 total) are chosen at random.

options.name: defense_increase
options.description:
Determines the number of Defense Increases to add to the multiworld.

The randomizer will add all stat ups defined here into a pool and choose up to 99 to add to the multiworld.

Accessory Slot Increases and Item Slot Increases are prioritized first, then the remaining items (up to 99 total) are chosen at random.

options.name: hp_increase
options.description:
Determines the number of HP Increases to add to the multiworld.

The randomizer will add all stat ups defined here into a pool and choose up to 99 to add to the multiworld.

Accessory Slot Increases and Item Slot Increases are prioritized first, then the remaining items (up to 99 total) are chosen at random.

options.name: ap_increase
options.description:
Determines the number of AP Increases to add to the multiworld.

The randomizer will add all stat ups defined here into a pool and choose up to 99 to add to the multiworld.

Accessory Slot Increases and Item Slot Increases are prioritized first, then the remaining items (up to 99 total) are chosen at random.

options.name: mp_increase
options.description:
Determines the number of MP Increases to add to the multiworld.

The randomizer will add all stat ups defined here into a pool and choose up to 99 to add to the multiworld.

Accessory Slot Increases and Item Slot Increases are prioritized first, then the remaining items (up to 99 total) are chosen at random.

options.name: accessory_slot_increase
options.description:
Determines the number of Accessory Slot Increases to add to the multiworld.

The randomizer will add all stat ups defined here into a pool and choose up to 99 to add to the multiworld.

Accessory Slot Increases and Item Slot Increases are prioritized first, then the remaining items (up to 99 total) are chosen at random.

options.name: item_slot_increase
options.description:
Determines the number of Item Slot Increases to add to the multiworld.

The randomizer will add all stat ups defined here into a pool and choose up to 99 to add to the multiworld.

Accessory Slot Increases and Item Slot Increases are prioritized first, then the remaining items (up to 99 total) are chosen at random.

options.name: max_level_for_slot_2_level_checks
options.display_name: Max Level for Slot 2 Level Checks
options.description: Determines the max level for slot 2 level checks.

options.name: keyblades_unlock_chests
options.description:
If toggled on, the player is required to have a certain keyblade to open chests in certain worlds.

TT   - Lionheart

WL   - Lady Luck

OC   - Olympia

DJ   - Jungle King

AG   - Three Wishes

MS   - Wishing Star

HT   - Pumpkinhead

NL   - Fairy Harp

HB   - Divine Rose

EotW - Oblivion

HAW  - Spellbinder

DI   - Oathkeeper

Note: Does not apply to Atlantica, the emblem and carousel chests in Hollow Bastion, or the Aero chest in Neverland currently.

options.name: keyblade_stats
options.description:
Determines whether Keyblade stats should be randomized.

Randomize: Randomly generates stats for each keyblade between the defined minimums and maximums.

Shuffle: Shuffles the stats of the vanilla keyblades amongst each other.

Vanilla: Keyblade stats are unchanged.

options.name: bad_starting_weapons
options.description: Forces Kingdom Key, Dream Sword, Dream Shield, and Dream Staff to have vanilla stats.

options.name: keyblade_min_str
options.description: Determines the minimum STR bonus a keyblade can have.

options.name: keyblade_max_str
options.description: Determines the maximum STR bonus a keyblade can have.

options.name: keyblade_min_crit_rate
options.description: Determines the minimum Crit Rate bonus a keyblade can have.

options.name: keyblade_max_crit_rate
options.description: Determines the maximum Crit Rate bonus a keyblade can have.

options.name: keyblade_min_crit_str
options.description: Determines the minimum Crit STR bonus a keyblade can have.

options.name: keyblade_max_crit_str
options.description: Determines the maximum Crit STR bonus a keyblade can have.

options.name: keyblade_min_recoil
options.description: Determines the minimum recoil a keyblade can have.

options.name: keyblade_max_recoil
options.description: Determines the maximum recoil a keyblade can have.

options.name: keyblade_min_mp
options.description: Determines the minimum MP bonus a keyblade can have.

options.name: keyblade_max_mp
options.description: Determines the maximum MP bonus a keyblade can have.

options.name: orichalcum_in_pool
options.description:
Determines how much Orichalcum, one of the two synthesis items, is in the item pool.

You need 17 to synth every recipe that requires it.

options.name: orichalcum_price
options.description: Determines the cost of Orichalcum in each shop.

options.name: mythril_in_pool
options.description:
Determines how much Mythril, one of the two synthesis items, is in the item pool.

You need 16 to synth every recipe that requires it.

options.name: mythril_price
options.description: Determines the cost of Mythril in each shop.

options.name: randomize_ap_costs
options.description:
Off: No randomization
Shuffle: Ability AP Costs will be shuffled amongst themselves.

Randomize: Ability AP Costs will be randomized to the specified max and min.

Distribute: Ability AP Costs will totalled and re-distributed randomly between the specified max and min.

options.name: max_ap_cost
options.description: If Randomize AP Costs is set to Randomize or Distribute, this defined the max AP cost an ability can have.

options.name: min_ap_cost
options.description: If Randomize AP Costs is set to Randomize or Distribute, this defined the min AP cost an ability can have.

options.name: randomize_puppies
options.description:
If OFF, the "Puppy" item is worth 3 puppies and puppies are placed in vanilla locations.

If ON, the "Puppy" item is worth an amount of puppies defined by "Puppy Value", and are shuffled randomly.

options.name: puppy_value
options.description: Determines how many dalmatian puppies are given when a puppy item is found.

options.name: starting_worlds
options.description:
Number of random worlds to start with in addition to Traverse Town, which is always available.

Will only consider Atlantica if toggled, and will only consider End of the World if its unlock is set to "Item".

These are given by the server, and are received after connection.

options.name: interact_in_battle
options.description: Allow Sora to talk to people, examine objects, and open chests in battle.

options.name: logic_difficulty
options.description:
Determines what the randomizer logic may expect you to do to reach certain locations.

Beginner: Logic only expects what would be the natural solution in vanilla gameplay or similar, as well as a guarantee of tools for boss fights.

Normal: Logic expects some clever use of abilities, exploration of options, and competent combat ability; generally does not require advanced knowledge.

Proud: Logic expects advanced knowledge of tricks and obscure interactions, such as using Combo Master, Dumbo, and other unusual methods to reach locations.

Minimal: Logic expects the bare minimum to get to locations; may require extensive grinding, beating fights with no tools, and performing very difficult or tedious tricks.

options.name: extra_shared_abilities
options.description: If on, adds extra shared abilities to the pool.  These can stack, so multiple high jumps make you jump higher and multiple glides make you superglide faster.

options.name: exp_zero_in_pool
options.description: If on, adds EXP Zero ability to the item pool.  This is redundant if you are planning on playing on Proud.

options.name: donald_death_link
options.description: If Donald is KO'ed, so is Sora.  If Death Link is toggled on in your client, this will send a death to everyone who enabled death link.

options.name: goofy_death_link
options.description: If Goofy is KO'ed, so is Sora.  If Death Link is toggled on in your client, this will send a death to everyone who enabled death link.

options.name: starting_tools
options.description:
Determines whether you start with Scan and Dodge Roll.

These are given by the server, and are received after connection.

options.name: remote_items
options.description:
Determines if items can be placed on locations in your own world in such a way that will force them to be remote items.

Off: When your items are placed in your world, they can only be placed in locations that they can be acquired without server connection (stats on levels, items in chests, etc).

Allow: When your items are placed in your world, items that normally can't be placed in a location in-game are simply made remote (abilities on static events, etc).

Full: All items are remote.  Use this when doing something like a co-op seed.

options.name: shorten_go_mode
options.description: If on, the player warps to the final cutscene after defeating Ansem 1 > Darkside > Ansem 2, skipping World of Chaos.

options.name: death_link
options.description:
If Sora is KO'ed, the other players with "Death Link" on will also be KO'ed.
The opposite is also true.

options.name: one_hp
options.description: If on, forces Sora's max HP to 1 and removes the low health warning sound.

options.name: four_by_three
options.description: If on, changes the aspect ratio to 4 by 3.

options.name: auto_attack
options.description: If on, you can combo by holding confirm.

options.name: beep_hack
options.description: If on, removes low health warning sound.  Works up to max health of 41.

options.name: consistent_finishers
options.description: If on, 30% chance finishers are now 100% chance.

options.name: early_skip
options.description: If on, allows skipping cutscenes immediately that normally take time to be able to skip.

options.name: fast_camera
options.description: If on, speeds up camera movement and camera centering.

options.name: faster_animations
options.description: If on, speeds up animations during which you can't play.

options.name: unlock_0_volume
options.description: If on, volume 1 mutes the audio channel.

options.name: unskippable
options.description: If on, makes unskippable cutscenes skippable.

options.name: auto_save
options.description:
If on, enables auto saving.

Press L1+L2+R1+R2+D-Pad Left to instantly load continue state.

Press L1+L2+R1+R2+D-Pad Right to instantly load autosave.

options.name: warp_anywhere
options.description:
If on, enables the player to warp at any time, even when not at a save point.

Press L1+L2+R2+Select to open the Save/Warp menu at any time.

options.name: randomize_party_member_starting_accessories
options.description:
If on, the 10 accessories that some party members (Aladdin, Ariel, Jack, Peter Pan, Beast) start with are randomized.

10 random accessories will be distributed amongst any party member aside from Sora in their starting equipment.

options.name: stacking_world_items
options.description:
Multiple world items give you the world's associated key item.

WL - Footprints

OC - Entry Pass

DJ - Slides

HT - Forget-Me-Not and Jack-In-The-Box

HB - Theon Vol. 6

Adds an extra world to the pool for each that has a key item (WL, OC, DJ, HT, HB).

Forces Halloween Town Key Item Bundle ON.

options.name: halloween_town_key_item_bundle
options.description:
Obtaining the Forget-Me-Not automatically gives Jack-in-the-Box as well.

Removes Jack-in-the-Box from the pool.

options.name: local_items
options.description: Forces these items to be in their native world.

options.name: non_local_items
options.description: Forces these items to be outside their native world.

options.name: start_inventory
options.description: Start with the specified amount of these items. Example: "Bomb: 1"

options.name: start_hints
options.description: Start with these item's locations prefilled into the ``!hint`` command.

options.name: start_location_hints
options.description: Start with these locations and their item prefilled into the ``!hint`` command.

options.name: start_inventory_from_pool
options.description:
Start with the specified amount of these items and don't place them in the world. Example: "Bomb: 1"

The game decides what the replacement items will be.
